message:
fix(cli): robust Cargo.toml name field parsing; fix io_driver test clone

cli/doctor: parse_package_name now uses split_once('=') to separate the
key from the value in Cargo.toml [package] name fields, instead of
chaining trim_start_matches("name") and trim_start_matches('='). The
previous approach would silently strip leading characters from the value
if the package name happened to start with characters in "name=" (e.g.,
a crate named "emanate" would have "e" stripped because
trim_start_matches works character-by-character, not as a prefix match).
split_once('=') correctly splits on the first equals sign regardless of
value content. A warning is now logged if the line has no '=' at all.

io_driver: test changed `let driver_clone = driver` (move) to
`let driver_clone = driver.clone()` to avoid moving the driver into the
spawned thread while it is still borrowed by the registration, which
would fail to compile when IoDriverHandle does not implement Copy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
